title: Multiply by 3 and 6
description: Multiply by 3 or 6 times a number less than or equal to 10.
topic_path: Module 3: Multiplication and division with units of 0, 1, 6–9, and multiples of 10
Topic A: The properties of multiplication and division

hints:
We can think of $\blueD{3} \times \greenD{1}$ as $\blueD3$ rows of $\greenD1$ circle. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{3} \times \greenD{1} = \purpleC{3}$

We can think of $\blueD{3} \times \greenD{3}$ as $\blueD3$ rows of $\greenD3$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{3} \times \greenD{3} = \purpleC{9}$

We can think of $\blueD{3} \times \greenD{4}$ as $\blueD3$ rows of $\greenD4$ circles. How many circles are there?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD3\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD4}\\
\\
&~~\Large\purpleC{12}
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{3} \times \greenD{7}$ as $\blueD3$ rows of $\greenD7$ circles. How many circles are there?



[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD3\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD7}\\
\\
&~~\Large\purpleC{21}
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{3} \times \greenD{10}$ as $\blueD3$ rows of $\greenD{10}$ circles. How many circles are there?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{3} \times \greenD{10} = \purpleC{30}$

\\
